title: udetailpos
text: udetailpos Return the coordinates of a double-click. short udetailpos (POINT* point ); Parameters point A structure of type POINT , (defined in windows.h ). The members x and y of this structure contain the coordinates of the double-click. (Output) Return Values Table 1. Values returned by udetailpos Value Description 0 The call was successful. -1 The call failed. This occurs if you did not get here via a double-click. Use Microsoft Windows platforms only. In library yrtl . Description udetailpos returns the x and y coordinates of the last double-click on a graphic image displayed in Uniface. The x and y coordinates are expressed in pixels, relative to the upper left-hand corner of the image. If the image is smaller than the field it is displayed in, it is possible to receive values that are outside the boundaries of the image. The coordinates are valid only if this function is called during processing of the detail trigger of an image field. In all other cases they will be invalid, and the function will return –1. In a Uniface form, define a field of type Image, and display an image in it. In the detail trigger of that field, call a 3GL function, for example perform "DOCLICK" . This 3GL function then calls udetailpos to process the mouse click further, for example: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <windows.h> EXPORT void DOCLICK() { POINT p; udetailpos(&p); u_printf("You clicked on x=%d, y=%d\n", p.x, p.y); /* Further actions dependent on x and y values */ } Copy Was this topic helpful?
